rationale: Factual trade-press report on leadership reshuffle across Kering houses, no evaluative verdict on Gucci itself.
Official Kering press release confirming a BV appointment; purely factual corporate announcement, not opinion about Gucci.
Financial-wire piece links Kering stock decline to Gucci's slow creative reset and margin pressure vs. stronger sister brands.
Ongoing labour-exploitation supply-chain probe keeps Gucci in critical Italian coverage, though no charges/wrongdoing found yet.
Official release on 50-year L'Oreal beauty licence, framed by analysts as a rare structural positive in Gucci's turnaround; magnitude capped as corporate-release origin.
Collector forum thread flags perceived quality slip since Tom Ford era, though some pushback exists, keeping it mild.
Forum reactions to Monte Carlo campaign genuinely split between praise and 'cheap and dated' criticism; no clear net polarity.
Brand-owned Instagram campaign post is descriptive activation coverage, not independent public opinion.
Quantified 14.3% Q1 revenue decline reported by WWD, weakest in key markets; clear negative earnings/momentum press.
Critical opinion piece frames Gucci's troubles as a design/positioning crisis, not just financial, echoing 'lost its way' narrative.
